w:r.w:t: Complete Prompts to Build This Project Using Any AI
You have a full project — NexusAgent — AI-Powered DeFi Risk Defense Infrastructure.
This guide gives you EXACT prompts to paste into Claude, ChatGPT, or any AI.
Each prompt is engineered to get maximum quality output for that specific task.
Follow the prompts in order. Each one builds on the previous.
You do not need to understand everything — just follow, run, and ship.
Tell AI exactly WHO it is
AI adopts expert persona — better output
No vague answers — AI knows your exact project
Tell AI what NOT to do
Prevents bloat, prevents wrong tech choices
Specify exactly how answer should look
Ask AI to reason step by step
Never mix two tasks in one prompt
Paste this at the START of EVERY new conversation with AI.
This gives the AI full context about your project.
After pasting this, you can ask any specific question and AI will understand the project.
Think of this as "loading your project into AI memory" at the start of each session.
THE PROMPT — Copy Everything Below This Line
You are a senior software engineer helping me build NexusAgent.
NexusAgent is an AI-Powered DeFi Risk Defense Infrastructure built for
HACKHAZARDS 2026 hackathon. It is NOT a trading bot. It is a SECURITY
and RISK INTELLIGENCE system for DeFi wallets.
"Autonomous AI risk intelligence layer that monitors DeFi wallets,
detects threats before funds are lost, executes protective actions."
- Layer 1: Deterministic Rule Engine (calculates risk with real math)
- Layer 2: Claude API (ONLY explains what the engine calculated — never calculates)
1. Wallet Health Score (0-100) based on 6+ risk signals
2. Rug Pull Detection Engine (analyzes token contracts)
3. Protocol Risk Monitor (TVL drops, liquidity anomalies)
5. AI Explainability Layer (Claude explains every risk decision)
6. Attack Simulation Mode (market crash, rug pull, depeg scenarios)
Frontend: Next.js 14, TailwindCSS, Framer Motion, Recharts, Wagmi, RainbowKit
AI: Claude API — claude-sonnet-4 — ONLY for explanation, not calculation
Network: Polygon Mumbai Testnet (free, fast, zero cost)
1. NexusDefender.sol — user defense profiles + action logging
Skill level: Intermediate Solidity developer, learning full-stack
Dark mode. Bloomberg terminal / cybersecurity SOC feel.
Professional, data-heavy, minimal. NO neon gamer UI.
1. Keep smart contracts SIMPLE — no complex DeFi protocols
2. Claude API is ONLY for explanation — rule engine does ALL calculations
3. Use only free tiers of all APIs
4. Every feature must be demo-able in the 3-minute presentation
5. No over-engineering — if it does not improve the demo, cut it
I will now ask you specific tasks. Answer as a senior engineer on this project.
WEEK 1 PROMPTS — Project Setup & Smart Contracts
Get exact commands to set up the entire project from zero.
You are a senior software engineer on the NexusAgent project.
TASK: Give me the exact terminal commands to:
1. Create the complete NexusAgent folder structure from scratch
2. Initialize npm in contracts/, backend/, and frontend/ folders
3. Install all required dependencies for each folder
4. Create the initial .env template files with all variable names
5. Initialize a Git repository and create a .gitignore that excludes .env files
- One bash script I can run top to bottom
- Add a comment above each command explaining what it does
- If a command requires user input, tell me exactly what to type
DO NOT explain concepts. ONLY give me runnable commands.
Get the complete smart contract code, ready to compile.
You are a senior Solidity developer on the NexusAgent project.
TASK: Write the complete NexusDefender.sol smart contract.
- Must extend OpenZeppelin Ownable and ReentrancyGuard
- Must have a DefenseProfile struct with these fields:
- Must have: createProfile(), updateProfile(), getProfile(), logDefenseAction()
- logDefenseAction() can only be called by contract owner
- Must emit events for: ProfileCreated, ProfileUpdated, DefenseTrigger
- Add NatSpec comments on every function
- Add require() validation on all inputs
- The complete Hardhat test file for this contract
- At least 8 test cases covering happy path and edge cases
FORMAT: Give contract code first, then test file. No explanations.
Get the approval revocation contract — your killer on-chain feature.
You are a senior Solidity developer on the NexusAgent project.
TASK: Write the complete ApprovalGuard.sol smart contract.
- Define a minimal IERC20 interface with only: approve() and allowance()
- User calls this themselves (msg.sender is the wallet owner)
- Must check that allowance > 0 before trying to revoke
- Emits ApprovalRevoked event with: user, token, spender, previousAllowance, timestamp
- Revoke multiple approvals in one transaction (max 20)
- Add complete Hardhat test file with 6+ tests
FORMAT: Contract first, then tests. No explanations.
Get deployment scripts ready for Polygon Mumbai testnet.
You are a senior Solidity developer on the NexusAgent project.
1. The complete hardhat.config.js for deploying to Polygon Mumbai testnet
- Must load environment variables with dotenv
- Must include Polygonscan for contract verification
2. The complete deploy.js script in contracts/scripts/
- Save addresses to a deployed.json file automatically
3. A verify.js script that reads deployed.json and verifies both contracts on Polygonscan
FORMAT: File name as comment on top of each code block. Commands at end.
WEEK 2 PROMPTS — Risk Engine & AI Layer
Get the complete Node.js + Express backend skeleton running.
You are a senior Node.js developer on the NexusAgent project.
TASK: Give me the complete backend setup including:
1. package.json with all required dependencies and dev dependencies
- Sets up CORS for frontend on localhost:3000
- Has a GET /health endpoint returning { status: ok, timestamp }
- Listens on PORT from env or 4000
- Logs startup message with port number
- Max 30 requests per minute per IP
- Checks that :address param is a valid Ethereum address
FORMAT: Filename comment on top of each block. Pure code, no explanations.
The core risk engine — builds the wallet health score from real data.
You are a senior Node.js developer building a DeFi risk engine.
This module calculates a wallet health score (0-100) using REAL deterministic logic.
No AI. No guessing. Pure math and rules.
Logic: (stablecoin USD value / total USD value) * 30
Stablecoins: USDC, USDT, DAI, BUSD, FRAX
Logic: If largest single token > 70% of portfolio = 0 pts
Logic: Subtract 5 points for each known risky protocol in wallet
Logic: Wallet active > 1 year = 5 bonus points
Logic: If total outflow last 7 days > 50% portfolio = subtract 15
signals: array of { name, score, maxScore, detail, isRisk },
Grade thresholds: SECURE=75+, MODERATE=50-74, AT RISK=25-49, CRITICAL=0-24
Use placeholder functions for data fetching — I will fill those in separately.
FORMAT: Pure code only. Export the function at the bottom.
The most impressive feature — token contract security analysis.
You are a senior blockchain security analyst and Node.js developer.
This module analyzes a token contract address and calculates a rug pull probability.
Uses ONLY deterministic rules — not AI.
Data needed: top holders list from Etherscan
Data needed: contract ABI from Etherscan
Logic: mint function exists AND unrestricted = +20
mint function exists but has onlyOwner = +10
Logic: detect upgradeTo(), implementation(), delegatecall in ABI
Logic: if top 10 wallets hold > 80% of supply = +15
Data needed: use a placeholder getLiquidityLock(tokenAddress)
Logic: NOT verified = +10 (unverified contracts are suspicious)
risks: array of { rule, signal, detail, points, severity },
Risk levels: LOW=0-30, MEDIUM=31-60, HIGH=61-80, CRITICAL=81-100
Use placeholder functions for all data fetching.
FORMAT: Pure code only. No explanations.
Wire up Claude API correctly — explanation only, never calculation.
You are a senior AI integration engineer.
CRITICAL RULE: Claude NEVER calculates risk. Claude ONLY explains
what the deterministic rule engine already calculated.
This is the architecture that separates us from "LLM wrapper" projects.
Input: the return object from calculateWalletHealthScore()
Prompt Claude with: score, grade, all signals, key risk flags
Tone: Direct, factual, non-alarmist. No financial advice.
Input: the return object from detectRugPull()
Prompt Claude with: rugPullScore, riskLevel, list of detected risks
Must mention the top 2 risk signals by name
Input: protocol name, TVL change percentage, anomaly type
Output: 2-sentence explanation of what the anomaly means for user funds
- Always explain WHY something is risky, not just that it is
- Use simple language — assume non-technical user
If Claude API fails, return a short fallback string built from the data.
App must never crash if AI is unavailable.
FORMAT: Pure code. Export all 3 functions.
WEEK 3–4 PROMPTS — Data Layer & API Routes
You are a senior Node.js developer.
Wrapper for Polygonscan API (same as Etherscan but for Polygon).
→ Returns list of unique ERC-20 tokens the wallet has interacted with
→ Returns top 20 holders with: address, quantity, percent
→ Returns parsed JSON ABI or empty array if not verified
→ Returns MATIC balance in ETH units (not wei)
→ Returns transactions from last N days
→ Include: hash, value, to, from, timestamp, isError
- All functions async, return clean parsed data
- Handle API errors — return empty array/null, never throw
- Add 300ms delay between requests to respect rate limits
- Log errors to console but do not crash
FORMAT: Pure code. Export all functions.
You are a senior Node.js developer.
Wrapper for DefiLlama API. No API key needed — fully free.
→ Returns: { name, currentTVL, tvlChange24h, tvlChange7d, chains, lastUpdated }
→ Flag as "ANOMALY" if tvlChange24h is worse than -15%
→ Returns top 50 DeFi protocols by TVL
→ Include: name, tvl, change24h, category, chains
→ Each result includes: name, tvl, change24h, anomalyType ("CRASH" or "SPIKE")
→ Returns top 20 yield opportunities on specified chain
→ Include: protocol, symbol, apy, tvlUsd, risk (low/medium/high)
FORMAT: Pure code. Export all functions.
You are a senior Node.js developer.
TASK: Write all 3 route files for the backend.
→ Validate tokenAddress is a valid ETH address
→ Returns token list with USD values
→ Returns list of active ERC-20 approvals (spending permissions)
→ Each includes: token, spender, allowance, riskLevel
{ name, description, portfolioImpact, newRiskScore, aiActions[], duration }
→ aiActions must be 3 realistic defense responses
- Use try/catch on every route — never let server crash
- Return consistent error format: { error: string, code: number }
- Add response time logging with console.log
FORMAT: Filename comment at top of each block. Pure code.
You are a senior frontend developer.
TASK: Give me the complete setup for the NexusAgent frontend.
- Global styles: background nexus-bg, text white
- Connect Wallet button using RainbowKit ConnectButton
- 3 feature cards below: Wallet Health, Rug Detection, Simulations
- Logo left: "NEXUS" in purple + "AGENT" in white
USE: Tailwind classes only — no custom CSS files.
FORMAT: Filename comment. Pure JSX. No explanations.
You are a senior React developer.
This is the HERO component — the first thing judges see.
It must look impressive and professional.
- When loading: show animated skeleton/pulse state
- When data arrives: score number animates from 0 to final value (use framer-motion)
- Left side: Giant score number (e.g., "74") with "/100" smaller
Colors: SECURE=green, MODERATE=amber, AT RISK=orange, CRITICAL=red
- Right side: "AI Analysis" box with dark purple background
Small "AI" badge in top corner of this box
- Bottom: Grid of signal cards (one per signal in data.signals)
Each card: signal name, score/maxScore bar, detail text
- Score number counts up from 0 to final value over 1.5 seconds
- Signal cards fade in staggered (0.1s delay each)
FORMAT: Pure JSX with Tailwind classes. Export as default.
You are a senior React developer.
- Input field where user pastes a token contract address
- Large circle with rug pull probability percentage
Each signal: icon, name, detail, points contribution
- AI explanation in separate box below
EXAMPLE TOKEN ADDRESSES TO SHOW AS PLACEHOLDERS:
- Show error if address is not a valid 42-char hex address starting with 0x
- Show error if API call fails
FORMAT: Pure JSX. Use fetch() for API calls. No axios.
You are a senior React developer.
This is your DEMO KILLER feature. Judges will interact with this.
Make it feel like a cybersecurity incident response dashboard.
- Title: "Attack Simulation Mode" with warning icon
- Subtitle: "Test how your wallet defenses respond to DeFi disasters"
- 3 scenario buttons (each with icon + name + short description):
2. "Rug Pull" — Token liquidity drained, emergency response
3. "Stablecoin Depeg" — USDC loses peg, rebalancing simulation
- Alert header: scenario name in red/amber
- New Risk Score badge (score changes visually)
Each item has: status icon, action text
Animate in one by one with 0.3s delay (like a terminal)
DESIGN: Dark background, red accent for danger scenarios.
Should feel like a security operations center dashboard.
FORMAT: Pure JSX. framer-motion for animations.
You are a senior Solidity developer.
I am getting this error when running npx hardhat compile:
Here is the contract that is failing:
1. Identify the exact cause of the error
2. Show me the fixed line(s) only — not the entire contract
3. Explain in ONE sentence why the fix works
4. Tell me if there are any other potential issues in the contract
PROMPT D.2 — API Not Returning Data
You are a senior Node.js developer.
My backend API endpoint is not returning data correctly.
What I get: [PASTE ACTUAL RESPONSE OR ERROR]
3. Tell me how to test it is working with a curl command
PROMPT D.3 — Frontend Not Connecting to Backend
You are a senior full-stack developer.
My Next.js frontend cannot connect to my Express backend.
1. Diagnose the exact problem (CORS? Wrong URL? Wrong method?)
2. Fix both frontend and backend code
3. Give me a checklist to verify the connection is working
PROMPT D.4 — MetaMask / Wallet Not Connecting
You are a senior Web3 frontend developer.
My wallet connection is not working.
3. Tell me how to add Polygon Mumbai network to MetaMask if not added
TASK: Give me step-by-step instructions to deploy the NexusAgent frontend to Vercel.
My frontend is a Next.js 14 app in the frontend/ subfolder of my monorepo.
My backend is a Node.js Express app — deployed separately.
1. Exact steps to deploy on Vercel (free tier)
2. How to set environment variables in Vercel dashboard
3. How to configure the root directory to frontend/
4. How to update NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL to point to production backend
5. How to check the deployment logs if it fails
6. The exact vercel.json file I need (if any)
TASK: Give me step-by-step instructions to deploy my Node.js Express
My backend is in the backend/ subfolder. It runs on port 4000.
2. How to set all my .env variables in Railway
3. How to get the public URL for my backend
4. What to put in Procfile or railway.json if needed
5. How to check logs if backend crashes after deployment
6. How to configure CORS for my Vercel frontend URL
You are a senior software engineer and technical writer.
TASK: Write a complete README.md for NexusAgent on GitHub.
JUDGES WILL READ THIS FIRST. Make it impressive.
1. Project banner area (text-based — no actual image required)
2. One-paragraph project description with the strong positioning
3. Key Features — with emoji icons, 6 features
4. Why NexusAgent? — the problem, with 1 statistic
6. Tech Stack — organized table by category
7. Getting Started — step by step setup from zero
8. Environment Variables — full table of all required vars
9. Demo — link placeholder + "How to test" instructions
LENGTH: Long enough to be impressive, not so long judges skip it.
FORMAT: Valid Markdown with proper headers, code blocks, tables.
You are a product demo specialist and hackathon coach.
TASK: Write a complete 3-minute demo script for NexusAgent.
I am presenting to hackathon judges who are experienced developers and investors.
They have seen many "AI crypto" projects. I need to stand out immediately.
I have a live working demo at a Vercel URL.
Must include one shocking statistic about DeFi losses.
No technical jargon. Appeal to emotion first.
3. The demo flow — exactly what I click and what I say
Include what to say WHILE clicking
4. How to handle the question: "Why use AI here?"
Give me a 3-sentence answer that shows technical depth.
5. How to handle the question: "Is the AI real?"
Explain the hybrid architecture in simple terms.
End with impact — what changes if this product exists.
FORMAT: Script format with [ACTION] and SPOKEN TEXT clearly separated.
ALWAYS start with Prompt 0 — Master Context
Set up project folder + install packages
Final Advice from a Prompt Engineer
1. ALWAYS paste Prompt 0 (Master Context) at the start of every new chat.
Without context, AI gives generic answers. With context, it knows your exact project.
2. ONE task per prompt. Never combine "write this AND also fix that".
Split it into two separate prompts for better quality.
3. If AI output is wrong or incomplete, say:
"That is not what I need. In NexusAgent, [explain what you expected].
4. If AI gives you an explanation instead of code, add this line:
"Do not explain. Give me ONLY the code I can copy-paste."
5. Always test the code before asking AI to extend it.
Broken code + new features = double broken code.
